picks.reasoning: Leicester have won just 1 of their last 15 Championship games — a genuinely catastrophic run for a side expected to be challenging near the top of the division. Despite this, Ladbrokes still price them at 1.55 (64.5% implied), apparently anchoring on Leicester's squad reputation rather than their current form. Sheffield Wednesday at home at 5.75 (17.4% implied) substantially understates the probability of a home win given the visiting side's collapse. A team winning 7% of their matches over a 15-game stretch should not be priced as near-65% certainties away from home. We rate Wednesday's true win probability at approximately 28.5%, giving the highest raw edge in today's pool.

Coventry are in strong form and pushing hard for promotion — Reddit sentiment from their own fanbase reads 'Premier League feels close now', suggesting genuine momentum in the camp. Hull are a mid-table side with no clear direction. Coventry's quality and current trajectory make Ladbrokes' 52.4% implied probability for an away win too conservative. We estimate their true win probability at 60%, representing a clear and well-backed edge.

Millwall are a notoriously difficult side to beat at The Den — one of the most intimidating atmospheres in the Championship. Norwich have suffered squad disruption this season with the high-profile mid-season departure of 16-year-old Ajay Tavares to Barcelona, leaving a gap in their attacking play. The combination of Millwall's strong home record and Norwich's unsettled squad makes Ladbrokes' 47.6% implied probability for a Millwall win too low. We estimate Millwall's true win probability at 54%.
